prerequisites: A Salesforce org with Marketing Cloud Account Engagement (Pardot) provisioned and at least one Account Engagement business unit configured.
A Salesforce admin able to create a connected app / external client app with OAuth enabled.
An SSO-enabled Salesforce user with Account Engagement access to authorize the connection and generate a refresh token.
The Account Engagement Business Unit ID (the 18-character ID beginning with 0Uv) for the pardot_business_unit_id field, found in Salesforce Setup under Business Unit Setup.
OAuth client credentials (Consumer Key and Consumer Secret) from the connected app for client_id and client_secret.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (refresh token)
authMethods.summary: The Account Engagement (Pardot) API authenticates with Salesforce OAuth 2.0. Create a connected app (external client app) with OAuth enabled and the Manage Pardot services / pardot_api scope, then authorize an SSO-enabled user to obtain a refresh token. Supply the app's Consumer Key as client_id, Consumer Secret as client_secret, and the long-lived refresh_token. The connector exchanges the refresh token for access tokens and sends them with the Pardot-Business-Unit-Id header on each request.

setupSteps.title: Create a connected app in Salesforce
setupSteps.body: In Salesforce Setup, open External Client Apps (External Client Manager) or App Manager, create a new app, and enable OAuth settings. Set a callback URL and add the Manage Pardot services scope (pardot_api). Save and wait up to 15 minutes for the app to become available.

setupSteps.title: Collect the client credentials
setupSteps.body: From the connected app, copy the Consumer Key into the client_id field and the Consumer Secret into the client_secret field. Both are required and stored as secrets.

setupSteps.title: Authorize and obtain a refresh token
setupSteps.body: Complete the OAuth Web Server flow with an SSO-enabled Salesforce user that has Account Engagement access to obtain a refresh token, and enter it in the refresh_token field.

setupSteps.title: Set the business unit ID
setupSteps.body: In Salesforce Setup, enter Business Unit in Quick Find and open Business Unit Setup to find the Account Engagement Business Unit ID (18 characters, begins with 0Uv). Enter it in pardot_business_unit_id. This value is sent in the required Pardot-Business-Unit-Id request header.

setupSteps.title: Configure optional settings
setupSteps.body: Set is_sandbox to true if you are connecting to a Salesforce sandbox so the connector targets the sandbox login endpoint. Optionally set start_date to bound the earliest records synced, and v5_page_size to tune the v5 API page size.
